examples.source: Like some other experts, he is skeptical about whether diabetes can be cured, noting that these findings have no relevance to people who already have Type 1 diabetes.
examples.reference: À l'instar d'autres experts, il se montre sceptique quant à la possibilité de guérir le diabète, faisant remarquer que ces résultats ne sont pas applicables aux personnes qui souffrent déjà de diabète de type 1.

Now, translating "Like some other experts" – in French, "Comme d'autres experts" seems right. "He is skeptical" could be "il est sceptique" or "il est sceptique". "About whether diabetes can be cured" – "sur la possibilité de la guérison de la diabète" might work. 

Next, "noting that these findings have no relevance" – "notant que ces résultats n'ont pas de relevé" or "notant que ces résultats ne sont pas pertinentes". "To people who already have Type 1 diabetes" – "à ceux qui ont déjà diabète type 1" or "à ceux qui ont déjà diabète type 1".

Wait, "relevé" might be better than "relevant" here. Also, "noting that" could be "notant que" or "indiquant que". Let me check the grammar again. "Notant que ces résultats n'ont pas de relevé à..." sounds correct.

examples.source: Dr. Ehud Ur, professor of medicine at Dalhousie University in Halifax, Nova Scotia and chair of the clinical and scientific division of the Canadian Diabetes Association cautioned that the research is still in its early days.
examples.reference: Ugqirha Ehud Ur, unjingalwazi wezonyango eYunivesithi yasee-Dalhousie e-Halifax, e-Nova Scotia ekwangusihlali wesebe lezophononongo kunye nesayensi kwi-Canadian Diabetes Association, ulumkise ngokuba uphando lusaqala.

examples.source: Like some other experts, he is skeptical about whether diabetes can be cured, noting that these findings have no relevance to people who already have Type 1 diabetes.
